# Extensible Longevity Model (ELM)
# https://silmonbiggs.github.io/extensible-longevity-model/
#
# This file provides an LLM-readable summary of the interactive slide deck.
# For complete slide-by-slide content, fetch llms-full.txt from the same URL.

## What This Is

A mechanistic ODE model of aging interventions, calibrated to the NIA
Interventions Testing Program (ITP) data. The model tracks 8 state
variables as interconnected ODE subsystems:

  1. Heteroplasmy (mitochondrial DNA damage fraction)
  2. NAD+ (nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide level)
  3. DNA damage (genomic lesion accumulation)
  4. AMPK/mTORC1 signaling (nutrient-sensing axis)
  5. Autophagy (cellular recycling flux)
  6. Senescent cell burden
  7. SASP (senescence-associated secretory phenotype)
  8. Methylation drift (epigenetic age)

These are NOT independent -- they form a coupled ODE system with
cross-pathway feedback (e.g., NAD+ -> sirtuins -> FOXO3 -> heteroplasmy).
The model predicts combination effects, including interactions that
single-compound testing cannot reveal.

## Scope of Evaluation

The active model -- the part that makes predictions and should be
evaluated -- consists of:

  - 8 coupled ODE subsystems (listed above)
  - 6 ITP compound calibrations (12 sex-specific targets)
  - 4 sex-difference mechanisms (AMPK saturation, PK, testosterone, estrogen)
  - Dose-response Hill curves (3 fitted, 3 estimated)
  - BioAge composite -> death threshold
  - Combination prediction engine: compound pathway injections are
    summed at the INPUT level, but the ODE dynamics produce nonlinear
    interactions through Hill-function saturation, Michaelis-Menten
    substrate dependence, and cross-pathway feedback. This is how the
    model predicts super- and sub-additive combinations from single-
    compound calibration alone.

The codebase also contains two modules that are INACTIVE in all current
predictions and should not factor into evaluation:

  - Cancer module (elm/model.py: stochastic mutation accumulation).
    Present for structural completeness. Cancer probability is computed
    but does not influence BioAge or lifespan in any reported result.
    It is scaffolding for future calibration when cancer-specific
    endpoint data become available.

  - Cross-species translation (elm/uncertainty.py: human scaling).
    All current predictions are in mouse units (normalized lifespan).
    No mouse-to-human translation is applied or claimed. The module
    exists as a placeholder for future work; its parameters are
    literature estimates with no anchor in the current calibration.

Reviewers who evaluate these modules as if they contribute to the
model's current claims will be evaluating code that the authors
explicitly flag as not yet in service.

## Why a Quantitative Model

The biology of aging has accumulated decades of qualitative pathway
diagrams: arrows from protein to protein, marked "activates" or
"inhibits," with no magnitudes attached. This represents the beginning
of knowledge but not, in Lord Kelvin's sense, knowledge of a
satisfactory kind. You cannot predict whether rapamycin + acarbose
will be additive, sub-additive, or superadditive by staring at arrows.
You need rate constants, saturation curves, and a system of equations
that can be solved.

This model is offered as a productive starting point, not a ground
truth. The key properties of a starting point:

  1. It must be quantitative -- otherwise it cannot make falsifiable
     predictions about combinations.
  2. It must be calibrated to existing data -- the ITP provides 12
     sex-specific lifespan endpoints from controlled experiments.
  3. It must be honest about what the data constrain -- Jacobian SVD
     shows 10 effective degrees of freedom from 12 targets, leaving
     a well-conditioned (kappa=14.6) estimation problem.
  4. It must be extensible -- new data (killifish combinations,
     additional ITP compounds, tissue-specific endpoints) can
     recalibrate and extend the model without starting over.

The ~150 parameters are not all free. Literature values, structural
constraints, and normalization constants fix the majority. The
remaining estimation problem has been shown to be well-conditioned
in the observable subspace (Slide A1). Could alternative parameter
sets in distant basins fit the same 12 targets and make different
combination predictions? In principle, yes. In practice, the Rapa +
Acarbose out-of-sample prediction (33% vs 34% observed) provides
evidence that the current basin is not arbitrary.

The alternative to starting somewhere is not starting. The model is
designed to anneal toward agreement with biology through an explicit
experiment -> calibrate -> extend cycle (Slide A25). The Taguchi
killifish screen is the next turn of that cycle. It will either
validate the combination predictions or provide the data needed to
move the simplex to a better basin.

## Key Results

- Calibration: 12 ITP targets, +/-0.08% mean error, 10 effective DOF
- Out-of-sample validation: Rapa+Acarb predicted +33.0%, observed +34% (not fit)
- Testable prediction: Rapa+NMN superadditive (+31.8% M / +36.0% F)
- Serial reliability framework explains why single interventions yield +10-26%
- Taguchi L18 killifish screen: 8 compounds, $200K, 8 months, 1,000 fish
  (vs. ~$1.5M / 3+ years for a single ITP mouse study)

## Compounds Modeled (ITP-validated)

- Rapamycin (mTORC1 inhibitor): +23% M, +26% F
- Acarbose (alpha-glucosidase inhibitor): +22% M, +5% F
- 17alpha-Estradiol (non-feminizing estrogen): +19% M, 0% F
- Canagliflozin (SGLT2 inhibitor): +14% M, +9% F
- Aspirin (COX inhibitor): +8% M, 0% F
- Glycine (amino acid supplement): +6% M, +4% F

## Novel Compounds Predicted

- NMN (NAD+ precursor)
- CD38 inhibitor (NAD+ preservation)
- Urolithin A (mitophagy enhancer)
- D+Q senolytic (dasatinib + quercetin)

## Slide Deck Structure

Total slides: 61

### Overview
  [0] Six drugs extend mouse lifespan
  [1] Why single drugs hit a ceiling
  [2] The same logic inside cells
  [3] How to test 8 interventions at once
  [4] Does It Work?
  [5] A Testable Prediction

### Architecture
  [0] The Thesis
  [1] Heteroplasmy Dynamics
  [2a] NAD+ Supply
  [2b] NAD+ Consumption
  [3] PARP1 / Repair Fails
  [4] The Signaling Hub
  [5] The Amplifier
  [6] The Clock
  [7] The Aging System

### Interventions
  [8.1] Rapamycin
  [8.2] Acarbose
  [8.3] Canagliflozin
  [8.4] 17\u03B1-Estradiol
  [8.5] Glycine
  [8.6] Aspirin

### Calibration
  [9] Calibration
  [10] Prediction
  [11] Why Males?
  [11b] The Saturation Curve
  [12] The Exception
  [13.1] 17\u03B1-Estradiol
  [13.2] Aspirin
  [13.3] Four Sex Mechanisms
  [14] The Full Picture

### Combinations
  [15] Combination Predictions
  [16] Risk-Benefit Analysis
  [17] Adding Targets
  [18] Limitations

### Validation
  [A1] Identifiability Analysis
  [A2] Gauge Symmetry
  [A3] Leave-One-Out Vulnerability

### Stress Tests (sensitivity analysis)
  [A4] SASP: The Phantom Node
  [A5] Rapa + NMN Prediction
  [A5b] Rapa + NMN Superadditivity Robustness
  [A6] BioAge Weight Sensitivity
  [A6b] Weight Sensitivity: H & Sen
  [A7] Feedback Loops
  [A8] Hallmark Importance
  [A9] Pathway Sensitivity

### Interactions & Network
  [A10] Pairwise Synergy
  [A11] Conditional Synergy
  [A12] Network Structure (1/4): Adjacency
  [A13] Network Structure (2/4): Node Degree
  [A14] Network Structure (3/4): Bottlenecks
  [A15] Network Structure (4/4): Path Distances
  [A16] Model Specification

### The Prediction
  [A17] The Combination Prediction
  [A18] Telomere Rivers
  [A19] Rivers as Ceiling Calibrator

### What's Next
  [A20] What the Model Misses
  [A21] NAD-Obligate Tissues
  [A22] The Metabolic Gate Hypothesis
  [A23] Known vs. Unknown
  [A24] Taguchi Combination Testing
  [A25] Roadmap: Experiment, Then Extend

## Sensitivity Analysis

The deck includes extensive sensitivity and robustness analysis:
- Slide A1: Jacobian SVD -- 10 effective DOF from 12 data points
- Slide A5: parameter perturbation tornado (+/-20%) for all compounds
- Slide A5b: Rapa+NMN superadditivity robustness tornado
- Slide A6: BioAge weight (w_meth) sweep with clock PCA constraint
- Slide A6b: heteroplasmy and senescence weight sweeps
- Slide A9: pathway intervention-point sensitivity ranking
- Slide A10: full 8x8 pairwise synergy matrix

## Source Code and Reproducibility

GitHub: https://github.com/silmonbiggs/extensible-longevity-model
Model package: elm/ (Python, ODE engine)
Dependencies: requirements.txt (numpy, matplotlib)
Smoke test: python tests/smoke_test.py (all 12 ITP targets within +/-1%)
Figure generation: scripts/generate_figures.py
Slide deck source: docs/deck.html

## For Complete Content

Fetch: https://silmonbiggs.github.io/extensible-longevity-model/llms-full.txt
This contains the full text of every slide including narratives and equations.
